todaysEvents.summary: Iran delivered MOU counter-proposal via Pakistan May 10; Trump: 'unacceptable'/'playing games'; Iran's sequencing demand: Lebanon ceasefire + Iranian oil export restrictions lifted first; nuclear programme and Hormuz framework deferred to 30-day follow-on talks (Al Jazeera, CNBC, IRNA, May 10). MOU framework stalled on sequencing gap; Pakistan continues to mediate; no scheduled follow-on talks confirmed.
todaysEvents.delta: Up: MOU framework effectively in stalemate - Iran's Lebanon-first demand and US nuclear-first position remain unresolved; blockade tightening with 70+ tankers blocked; tail risk raised to 40% from 35%

todaysEvents.summary: US blockade tightens: 70+ tankers blocked from entering or leaving Iranian ports (US CENTCOM May 13-14); 37 Iranian oil tankers trapped in Persian Gulf west of Hormuz; IEA warns 14M bpd Gulf supply cut (Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain) is depleting global inventories at record pace (IEA May 14); OFAC 'Economic Fury' action May 11 targets IRGC oil ops (ZEUS LOGISTICS GROUP, COSTIN SHIPPING, MIHIR SHIPPING).
todaysEvents.delta: Up: Economic warfare on both sides intensifying; Iran's oil sector under blockade pressure; global supply shock accelerating; Brent rallied from $101 (D67) to $110.87 peak (D72, Fortune confirmed) before Trump-Xi partial relief

todaysEvents.summary: Trump-Xi meeting May 14 discussed Hormuz; China urged de-escalation; Brent fell from $110.87 (D72 peak, Fortune) to $105.87 (CNBC May 14 confirmed); WTI ~$101 est.; Pakistan mediating; Iran says response was 'realistic and positive'; no scheduled follow-on talks confirmed; ceasefire nominally intact (May 14-15).
todaysEvents.delta: Down: Trump-Xi Hormuz discussion adds indirect pressure on Iran; China's economic interest to push for de-escalation; Brent pullback signals market not pricing imminent kinetic escalation; MOU gap remains but Trump-Xi creates new diplomatic channel

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Iran counter-proposal (Lebanon ceasefire + oil exports first; nuclear/Hormuz in 30-day follow-on) provides a sequenced negotiation path; Pakistan mediating; Trump-Xi Hormuz discussion (May 14) adds indirect pressure on Iran. If sequencing gap bridged, Brent retreats toward $88-95/bbl on confirmed deal framework; iTraxx tightens toward 165bp. Narrow (10%) - Trump declared counter-proposal 'unacceptable'; nuclear-sequencing gap unresolved; 70+ tankers blocked; no confirmed follow-on talks.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Prolonged standoff: talks continue via Pakistan on sequencing gap; Trump-Xi keeps indirect pressure on Iran; blockade drains Iranian economy while global supply depletes at record pace; oil stabilises $95-115/bbl; no imminent deal or full collapse. TTF €44-52/MWh; JKM $15-20/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 180-220bp. Malaysia June supply gap persists; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Dominant scenario (50%).

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: MOU sequencing gap becomes pretext for full collapse: Trump ends ceasefire / resumes strikes after Iran counter-proposal deadlock; IRGC resumes full Hormuz interdiction; blockade escalates to seizure operations. Brent spikes $125-170/bbl; Kharg Island seizure or GCC production targeting brings Saudi/UAE output under acute threat; Malaysia July-August crisis; iTraxx spikes to 230-260bp. Tail (40%) - raised from D67 35%; Iran's nuclear-first rejection + 70+ tankers blocked + IEA record depletion signal hard stalemate.
